ddls:
CREATE TABLE "actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each action */,
  "action_name" TEXT /* Name of the action (e.g., add, remove) */,
  "action_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the action was performed on */,
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interface the action was performed on */,
  "action_status" TEXT /* Status of the action (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action */,
  "alert_generated" INTEGER /* Indicates if an alert was generated for the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_interface_id FOREIGN KEY ("interface_id") REFERENCES interfaces ("interface_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., server, switch) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Planned date for next maintenance */,
  "maintenance_status" TEXT /* Current maintenance status (e.g., due, not due) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, network engineer) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* User's permissions (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interfaces" (
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interface */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the interface belongs to */,
  "interface_name" TEXT /* Name of the interface (e.g., eno0, eno1) */,
  "subsystem" TEXT /* Subsystem of the interface */,
  "kernel" TEXT /* Kernel identifier of the interface */,
  "driver" TEXT /* Driver used by the interface */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the interface */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the interface */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the interface (e.g., up, down) */,
  "last_update_time" TEXT /* Last update time of the interface */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the interface */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interface_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interfaces_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interfaces_kernel FOREIGN KEY ("kernel") REFERENCES kernels ("kernel_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interfaces_driver FOREIGN KEY ("driver") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interfaces_subsystem FOREIGN KEY ("subsystem") REFERENCES subsystems ("subsystem_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "drivers" (
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each driver */,
  "driver_name" TEXT /* Name of the driver */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the driver */,
  "compatible_devices" TEXT /* Device types compatible with the driver */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the driver */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the driver */,
  "support_link" TEXT /* Link to the driver's support documentation */,
  "download_link" TEXT /* Link to download the driver */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update time of the driver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subsystems" (
  "subsystem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subsystem */,
  "subsystem_name" TEXT /* Name of the subsystem (e.g., net) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subsystem */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the subsystem */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update time of the subsystem */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subsystem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "kernels" (
  "kernel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each kernel */,
  "kernel_name" TEXT /* Name of the kernel identifier (e.g., 0000:00:00.0) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the kernel identifier */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the kernel */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update time of the kernel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("kernel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "action_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "action_id" INTEGER /* ID of the action being logged */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the action was performed on */,
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interface the action was performed on */,
  "action_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log entry was created */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Detailed message of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the log (e.g., user, system) */,
  "additional_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_action_logs_action_id FOREIGN KEY ("action_id") REFERENCES actions ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_action_logs_interface_id FOREIGN KEY ("interface_id") REFERENCES interfaces ("interface_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_action_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_action_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)
